titleStmt.title: شرح على آداب البحث والمناظرة
Sharḥ ʿalā Ādāb al-Baḥth wa-al-Munāẓarah
Commentary on the Protocols of Inquiry and Debate

author.persName: تقي الدين محمد بن پير علي البركوي
Taqī al-Dīn Muḥammad ibn Pīr ʿAlī al-Birgivī

availability.p: This TEI file is shared for reuse under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0. The TEI encoding is designed for FAIR-compliant discovery, citation, and computational reuse.

msItem.title: شرح على آداب البحث والمناظرة
Sharḥ ʿalā Ādāb al-Baḥth wa-al-Munāẓarah
Commentary on the Protocols of Inquiry and Debate
msItem.note: Ultra-brief pedagogical digest or compressed commentary witness.
The text condenses the duties of the debater, premise-objection, refutation, counter-proof, transition to a new proof, and the procedural reversal of roles between objector and claimant.

supportDesc.extent: 1 folio (1 written side in the encoded primary witness)

handDesc.handNote:
Nastaʿliq:
A flowing slanted script associated with Ottoman scholarly manuscript culture. In this witness it suits the compressed pedagogical layout of the text.

condition.p: No explicit deterioration or damage in the file.

provenance.p: There is no explicit provenance information.

editorialDecl.p: The source file preserves two parallel transcriptions. The present TEI encodes only the cleaner primary witness, from the opening basmala to the closing phrase وقس عل الى ان ينتهى.
The second transcription, which restarts with a new basmala and exhibits dense OCR/HTR noise, is treated as an excluded duplicate witness rather than part of the edited body.
Lineation from the local edited transcription is represented by numbered lb elements. The English translation is aligned line-by-line to the encoded Arabic witness for research convenience.
AI-assisted English translation for research use; it should be absolutely checked against the Arabic witness.

projectDesc.p: This repository encodes a pilot corpus of early modern Arabic argumentation manuscripts in TEI-XML for manuscript description, semantic markup, and future corpus-scale DH analysis.

person.persName: تقي الدين محمد بن پير علي البركوي
Taqī al-Dīn Muḥammad ibn Pīr ʿAlī al-Birgivī

abstract.p: A compressed Ottoman teaching witness that reduces debate theory to procedural roles, objection-types, response-types, and the logic of reversal between claimant and objector.Author style is markedly mnemonic and schematic.

div.p:
بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
الحمدلله رب العالمين والصلاةوالسلام على حبيبه محمد وآله أجمعين وبعد فاعلم أن المناظر إذا نقل طلب
الصحة فقط إن لم يعرفها وإن ادعاها فيجوز الدليل أو التنبيه كذلك فإن أتى به منع بعض مقدماته كلها مجردا أو مع سند
فیسمئ يعرفها مناقضة ونقضا تفصيليا أو مع دليل فيسمى غصبا ففي قوله خلاف ودفعه باثبات المقدمة الممنوعة
أو نفي السند بالدليل أو بالتنبيه إن أمكن وإلا فبالانتقال إلى دليل آخر عند البعض وإن عجز فالإفحام أو منع الدليل
استلزامه فسادا كالتخلف ونحوه فيسمى نقضا إجماليا فينقلب الحال فيصير الخصم مدعيا والمناظر سائلا فله
المناقضة إن أمكن وإلا فله بالانتقال بلا انقلاب وإلا فحاله الإلزام أو منع المدلول بدلیل فيسمى معارضة
فينقلب أيضا فله دفعه بالمناقضة إن أمكن وإلا فله بالانتقال دليل آخر من متبين اخر بلاانقلاب
اذولو كان من جنس الأول صار معارضة فلا يفيد عند الحنفية إذ لا ترجيح عندهم بالأصل وكثرة الأجزاء
والأدلة وإنما هو بالوصف والقوة فإن عجز فله السكوت أو الاعتراف ثم في إثبات الممنوعة والانتقال
والانتقال للخصم المناقضة والنقض والمعارضة
والاخيران مناقضة بالنسبة
ال الدليل لا بدّ من شاهد
وقس عل الى ان ينتهى

div.note: The raw file then restarts with a second basmala and a noisier parallel witness. That duplicate transcription is documented in companion files but excluded from the main TEI body.

div.p:
In the name of God, the Compassionate, the Merciful.
Praise be to God, Lord of the worlds, and blessings and peace upon His beloved Muhammad and all his family. After that, know that when the debater merely transmits,
only verification is sought if it is not already known; but if he claims it as his own, then proof or indication is permitted. If, once he presents it, some or all of its premises are denied, whether merely or together with a ground,
that is called contradiction and also detailed refutation; but if it is with a proof, it is called procedural usurpation. There is disagreement about this term, and its rebuttal consists in establishing the denied premise,
or negating the ground by proof or by indication if possible; otherwise by moving to another proof according to some. If he is unable, then there is silencing the proponent. Or if one denies the proof
on the grounds that it entails corruption, such as failure of entailment and the like, this is called summary refutation. Then the situation is reversed, so that the opponent becomes the claimant and the debater the questioner, and he may then use
contradiction if possible; otherwise he may move to another proof without reversal. Otherwise his state is compelling the opponent. Or one may deny the conclusion by a proof, and this is called counter-proof.
Then the roles are reversed again, and he may repel it by contradiction if possible; otherwise he may move to another proof drawn from a different clarifying basis, without reversal.
For if it were of the same kind as the first, it would become counter-proof and would not avail according to the Hanafis, since preference for them is not established by origin or by the multiplicity of parts
and proofs; rather, it rests on quality and strength. If he is unable, then he may remain silent or make an admission. Then, in establishing the denied premise and in transition,
the opponent in turn has contradiction, refutation, and counter-proof.
And the latter two are also a kind of contradiction relative
to the proof, for which a witness is required.
Apply analogy to this until the sequence reaches its end.
